title: Dialectics Checklist

Interpersonal Effectiveness Worksheet 11a   (I. E. Handouts 15, 16)
Dialectics Checklist
Due Date:             Name:                                    Week Starting:
Everyday dialectical practice: Check off dialectical practice exercises each time you do one. For
each skill you practice, give it a rating to indicate how effective that skill was in helping you reach
your personal and interpersonal goals. Rate from a low of 1 (not at all effective) to a high of 5 (very
effective).
Looked at both sides:
Rating
(1–5)
  1.  Asked Wise Mind: “What am I missing?”

  2.  Looked for the kernel of truth in another person’s side.

  3.  Stayed away from extremes (such as “always” or never”), and instead thought
or said:


  4.  Balanced opposites in my life:   Validated both myself and a person
I disagreed with   Accepted reality and tried to change it   Stayed
attached and also let go   Other (describe):

  5.  Made lemonade out of lemons (describe):


  6.  Embraced confusion (describe):

  7.  Played devil’s advocate by arguing both my side and also the other side
(describe):


  8.  Used a metaphor or story to describe my own point of view (describe):


  9.  Did 3-minute Wise Mind to slow down “doing mind” in my everyday life.

10.  Other (describe):

Stayed aware of my connection:
11.  Treated others as I want to be treated (describe):

12.  Looked for similarities between myself and others (describe):

13.  Noticed the physical connections between all things (describe):

14.  Other (describe):

Embraced change:
15.  Practiced radical acceptance of change (describe):

16.  Purposely made changes in small ways to get used to change (describe):


17.  Other (describe):

Remembered that change is transactional:
18.  Paid attention to my effect on others (describe):

19.  Paid attention to effect of others on me (describe):

20.  Practiced letting go of blame (describe):

21.  Reminded myself that all things, including all behaviors, are caused

22.  Other (describe):

ourspace_shared_life_filter.title: Dialectics Checklist
ourspace_shared_life_filter.shared_facing_summary: Use this to protect both people: needs are real, boundaries are kind, and a pause is not abandonment when repair is named.
tiny_steps: Name the ask in one sentence.
Agree on a return time or next check-in.

flare_day_versions.minimum: One breath, one label, one safe message, or one checkbox.
flare_day_versions.medium: Two to five minutes with everything reachable from bed/chair.
flare_day_versions.full: Use the original worksheet/handout when both bodies and brains have capacity.

accessibility_modifications: Use text/chat if speech is hard.
Include caregiver capacity as part of the plan, not an afterthought.
relationship_and_caregiver_notes: William's needs and Jasper's capacity both count.
Ask before helping; ask before escalating; name the smallest next step.
Pause means pause, not abandonment. I am coming back. I care about us. I need a smaller next step.
What is needed, what capacity exists, what can be smaller, and what can wait?
identity_and_trauma_filter: Use William's masculine language and Jasper's they/them language.
Use consent-based, non-shaming, body-neutral language unless a person has explicitly chosen a term.
Do not turn symptoms, dysphoria, trauma responses, clutter, fatigue, or emotional intensity into moral failure.
avoid_or_adjust: Do not use skills to win, corner, threaten leaving, or make either person prove pain or disability.
